searchText: Arm Care Pitch Count Rules Faq Arm Care & Pitch Count Rules FAQ Arm Care & Pitch Count Rule FAQ Skip to main content Arm Care & Pitch Count Rules FAQ – Arm Care COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER Arm Care & Pitch Count Rules FAQ Updated for the 2026 baseball season Arm Care & Pitch Count Rule FAQ "How do I..." - A full guide to the Pitch Count App Where can I find information on the Arm Care rules? What are the pitch count limits and days rest requirements for each division? What happens when a pitcher reaches their limit? Can pitchers pitch on multiple games on the same day? Can pitchers pitch two days in a row? Can pitchers pitch three days in a row? Can a player pitch and catch in the same game/day? Who is responsible for entering the pitch count and when? Do pitch counts have to be entered live in-game, or can they be entered after the game? What if I don't have Wi-Fi or data at the park? What should the home team do if either team has reached their pitch count limit? What are my obligations when traveling outside of Ontario or Canada? What penalties apply when a team violates the pitch count rules? What happens when a team is not in the app? What happens when a player is not in the app? There's an error on my roster in the app, how can I fix it? How do I submit a trouble ticket for the Pitch Count app? "HOW DO I...?" A FULL GUIDE TO THE PITCH COUNT APP Have you ever wondered how to do something on the Pitch Count app? OBA has created a full series of How-To videos and Frequently Asked Questions on using the Pitch Count App. Please visit coachoba.ca for all the how-to and FAQ you will need! [TOP] WHERE CAN I FIND INFORMATION ON THE ARM CARE RULES? The full Arm Care rules can be found in the Baseball Ontario ONDeck app in the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules under Section A12. OBA Playing Rules [TOP] WHAT ARE THE PITCH COUNT LIMITS AND DAYS REST REQUIREMENTS FOR EACH DIVISION? Day 1 of the rest period is the day after the game the pitcher last pitched. Days rest is calculated based on the total number of pitches thrown. For example, if an 11U pitcher throws 65 pitches on Friday, their days rest would be 3-days and they would be permitted to next pitch again on Tuesday. "Last Batter" can be declared prior to the threshold being reached and any pitches thrown after the threshold are not counted. Example: The 15U threshold for zero days rest is 35 pitches. The pitcher begins the new batter at 32 pitches. Prior to the 35th pitch, the Manager indicates that this shall be the last batter for the pitcher. The pitcher is permitted to complete the current batter and a final pitch count of 35 is recorded. [TOP] WHAT HAPPENS WHEN A PITCHER REACHES THEIR LIMIT? When a pitcher reaches their pitch count limit during a game, and they are in the middle of an at-bat, the pitcher is permitted to continue to pitch until that batter is retired (under most circumstances). Please see Can Pitchers Pitch Three Days in a Row? below for the exception to this rule. When a pitcher reaches the maximum number of pitches allowed for any days-rest threshold, they may complete the at-bat without affecting their days rest, as long as they do not pitch to another batter. In this situation, the number of pitches corresponding to the threshold will be indicated on the pitching log (not the actual pitch count). The coach or manager must acknowledge this with the umpire prior to the threshold pitch being thrown. The umpire would then notify the official scorekeeper. Example: The 15U threshold for zero days rest is 35 pitches. The pitcher begins the new batter at 32 pitches. Prior to the 35th pitch, the Manager indicates that this shall be the last batter for the pitcher. The pitcher is permitted to complete the current batter and a final pitch count of 35 is recorded. At the end of the at-bat, the pitcher must be removed from the pitching position. [TOP] CAN PITCHERS PITCH IN MULTIPLE GAMES ON THE SAME DAY? Yes, pitchers can pitch in 2 games (max) on the same day as long as they don’t exceed their no rest required threshold in the first game of the day. The pitch count for both games will be added together to calculate days rest required. For example, a 15U pitcher could throw 25 pitches in game 1 of a doubleheader and in game 2 can pitch again because they haven’t exceeded their no-days rest threshold. They start game 2 at pitch 26 and can throw a total of 95 pitches combined over the two games. [TOP] CAN PITCHERS PITCH TWO DAYS IN A ROW? Yes, pitchers can pitch on two consecutive days as long as they haven’t exceeded their no rest required threshold on the first day and have only pitched in one game on the first day. On Day 2, the pitcher starts the day with the same number of pitches thrown on Day 1. So, for example, if a pitcher threw 20 pitches on Friday, they can pitch again on Saturday but their pitch count for the day starts at 21, not at zero. Pitchers can not pitch in more than 2 games in any 2-day period. In all cases, the pitch count for all games are added together to calculate days rest required. [TOP] CAN PITCHERS PITCH THREE DAYS IN A ROW? Yes, pitchers can pitch on three consecutive days as long as they do not exceed their no rest required threshold in all three games combined. For example, at 13U, a pitcher could throw 10 pitches each day on Friday, Saturday, and Sunday; as long as the pitcher does not exceed 30 pitches over the three consecutive days, this is permitted. If a pitcher throws on three consecutive days, they must rest for 1 day before they are eligible to pitch again. [TOP] CAN A PLAYER PITCH AND CATCH IN THE SAME GAME/DAY? No. Players can not pitch and catch in the same day regardless of which position they started in. Once a player has assumed the pitching position, they are no longer permitted to catch for the remainder of the day. Once a player has assumed the catching position, they are no longer permitted to pitch for the remainder of the day. [TOP] WHO IS RESPONSIBLE FOR ENTERING THE PITCH COUNT? The Home Team is always responsible for entering the pitch count for both teams. The Head Coach is accountable for the pitch count and responsible to ensure that it is entered. However, the coach may delegate the pitch count to a trusted assistant coach, team manager, trainer, or other volunteer. [TOP] WHEN DO PITCH COUNTS HAVE TO BE ENTERED? DO PITCH COUNTS HAVE TO BE ENTERED LIVE IN-GAME OR CAN THEY BE ENTERED AFTER THE GAME? In Rep and Select tournaments, pitch counts must be entered in the app in real-time during the game (either pitch-by-pitch or at the end of each half-inning). During regular season play it is permitted to enter pitch counts after the game as long as they are entered within 24 hours. However, OBA strongly recommends pitch-by-pitch tracking in the app for all games to ensure that coaches are notified of potential violations before they occur to avoid penalties to their team. Pitch-by-pitch tracking also displays real-time pitch counts, pitches remaining, and dramatically reduces the volume of data entry errors versus entering pitch counts post-game. [TOP] WHAT IF I DON'T HAVE ACCESS TO WI-FI OR DATA AT THE PARK? The Pitch Count app works without Wi-Fi or mobile data. Simply track your pitches as you normally would and submit the final pitch count as soon as you reconnect to Wi-Fi or data. The app uses minimal data since it's not transmitting large amounts of information. [TOP] WHAT SHOULD THE HOME TEAM DO IF EITHER TEAM HAS REACHED THEIR PITCH COUNT LIMIT? If pitch counts are being recorded pitch-by-pitch, the pitch counter will receive alerts, the screen will turn ORANGE on their device when a player is approaching and has reached their maximum pitch count. As it is the Home team's responsibility to capture the count, it is also the responsibility for the Home team's Manager/Head Coach to notify the plate umpire when the opposing team’s pitcher has reached their pitch count limit as soon as the screen on the app turns RED. The umpire must notify the Away Team Head Coach. The Head Coach is responsible for reporting to the umpire regardless of if they have delegated the pitch count responsibilities to someone. The Away team has the right to request the pitch count for either team at any time throughout the game to ensure that the count is accurate, and rules have not been violated. [TOP] WHAT ARE MY OBLIGATIONS WHEN TRAVELING OUTSIDE OF ONTARIO OR CANADA? All Arm Care rules are mandatory when traveling outside Ontario or Canada. Games played in US or other out-of-province territories must be entered into Pitch Count with no opponent selected within 24 hours after the completion of each game. Sanctions apply for coaches who fail to report Pitch Counts, exceed thresholds, or do not follow required Days Rest periods while playing games out of province. [TOP] WHAT PENALTIES APPLY WHEN A TEAM VIOLATES THE PITCH COUNT RULES? Different penalties apply depending on which team violates the pitch count rule, whether or not the umpire was notified, as well as if and when the violation was reported. TEAM VIOLATION VIOLATION REPORTED PENALTY Home Team violates pitch count In game Forfeit + 1 game suspension for Head Coach Home Team violates pitch count Post-game No forfeit 2 game suspension for Head Coach Away Team violates pitch count after notification by Official Pitch Counter to umpire at time of infraction In game Forfeit + 1 game suspension for Head Coach Away Team violates pitch count with NO notification to umpire prior to the time of infraction In game No forfeit Head Coach is ejected + 1 game suspension for Head Coach Away team violates pitch count Post-game No forfeit 2 game suspension for Head Coach IMPORTANT NOTE ON ENFORCEMENT: The intent of these rules is to enforce the pitch count rules and not to promote forfeits. The Home Team is responsible for the pitch count and therefore the duty of responsibility falls upon them to notify the opposing team of a pitcher reaching their pitch count via the plate umpire. Regardless of whether or not, the Home Team Head Coach delegates the pitch count to someone else, they are ultimately responsible and accountable to the Pitch Count. [TOP] WHAT HAPPENS IF A TEAM IS NOT IN THE APP? If a team is not in the app, it means that their roster has not yet been submitted and the team is not permitted to play. After May 1st, any team playing without having submitted a roster shall be automatically suspended until their roster is submitted. [TOP] WHAT HAPPENS IF A PLAYER IS NOT ON THE TEAM IN THE APP? If a player is not on the team in the app, they are not permitted to pitch. Call-up players can be added only by the team they are playing on before the game starts. Once the game starts, they can’t be added to the team in the app and are therefore not allowed to pitch. [TOP] THERE'S AN ERROR ON MY ROSTER, HOW CAN I FIX IT? Pitch Count rosters are pulled directly from the BaseballShift rostering system. If there is an error on your roster in Pitch Count, it is highly likely the result of incorrect data on your BaseballShift roster. Please reach out to your Local Registrar to have your roster corrected. Some common issues include: Missing player: This is a result of the player either not being entered into your BaseballShift roster, or the player does not have a valid birthdate on the roster. Incorrect days rest applied: This is usually the result of a player having an incorrect birthdate on the roster. Caution should be taken when selecting an existing player in the rostering system to reduce duplication of players on multiple teams. Missing or wrong coach: Coaches are required to have their email address and NCCP number on their roster. Pitch Counter / Team Manager Not On App: Only staff entered with valid email addresses will be given access to the app. If you wish your Team Manager to manage Pitch Counts for you, you must select Pitch Counter in the drop down menu on the BaseballShift roster. Note: this gives any staff full access to your Pitch Count app. I'm a coach and my name appears on my team in the app, but I haven't received my login: It is likely that your email address has been entered incorrectly on your roster (or a different one than you're using). Please see below for information on submitting a trouble ticket to Pitch Count Support. [TOP] HOW DO I SUBMIT A TROUBLE TICKET FOR THE PITCH COUNT APP? If you encounter a bug, have feedback on the app, or have a question for Pitch Count Support, please email pitchcountapp@baseballontario.com BEFORE YOU EMAIL: Be sure to email from the email address associated with your Pitch Count app if possible. Always include your Team ID which can be found in the URL for your BaseballShift roster (see below). If your question is in regard to a specific game, please include the game number. If your question is in regard to a specific player or coach, please include their full name. [TOP] Last Updated: 29-July-2024 SearchMember DirectoryEventsEmail Support Copyright © 2026 New Start Mobile, LLC https://ondeck.baseballontario.com/page/784/arm-care/16475/arm-care-pitch-count-rules-faq

Guidelines for Affiliated Associations Appendix B - Affiliated Associations Appendix C - National Championship Player Drafts RP1.1 Age and Category To be eligible to play in a particular age category, a player must not have a birthday which makes them older than the number in the series name. ie: A player in 9U (9 and Under) must not turn 10 dururing the current calendar year. CATEGORY/SERIES AGE OF PLAYER AS OF DECEMBER 31st OF THE CALENDAR YEAR 8U Not older than 8 years of age but not younger than 6 as of December 31st 9U Not older than 9 years of age 10U Not older than 10 years of age 11U Not older than 11 years of age 12U Not older than 12 years of age 13U/13U Girls Not older than 13 years of age 14U Not older than 14 years of age 15U Not older than 15 years of age 16U/16U Girls Not older than 16 years of age 18U Not older than 18 years of age 22U Not older than 22 years of age Senior/Women’s Unlimited but not younger than 17 as of December 31st [TOP] RP1.2 Birth Records All players 21 years of age and under must produce a birth record the first time they register with OBA. Only birth certificates, passports, citizenship cards or driver’s licenses will be accepted as birth records. In the case of Affiliated Associations having their own registrar and having the approval of the OBA, it is not necessary to send birth records to the OBA Office if such birth records are provided to the Affiliated Association’s registrar and will be recorded by the registrar. For Affiliated Associations not having their own registrar or for Direct Entries, when sending birth records to the OBA Office, they must be listed, and a copy of the list included with the birth records. Upon receipt of a birth record, it will be recorded in the OBA Office. Players who cannot procure a birth record may sign a team roster upon producing a statutory declaration sworn by their parent or guardian establishing their age; the same to be accompanied by a certificate of a solicitor or notary public. [TOP] RP1.3 Residency Rules The basic premise of the Rep Division’s residence rule is that players are to play with the Local Association where they reside. Players must establish residence within the Local Association on or before June 30th of the playing year. Exception: a player, who (1) maintains Canadian citizenship, (2) has been a member of a Rep Local Association or Direct Entry for at least one year prior, (3) continues to be a member of the same Rep Local Association or Direct Entry, and (4) for any reason is living outside of the Province of Ontario, may continue to register with that Local Association or Direct Entry. Individuals may be granted permission by the Rep Registrar to participate in Rep play. Cases will be dealt with on an individual basis (i.e., exchange students). Affiliated Associations must establish territorial boundaries for Local Associations within the Affiliated Association. Affiliated Associations may also set policies applicable within their Affiliated Association. A copy of the boundaries and any related policies must be filed with Baseball Ontario. The Rep Division will consider the most recent copies filed by the Affiliated Association in any dispute. The Rep Division may, in its discretion, choose to not follow the Affiliated Association policy where it conflicts with the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and/or the Procedures and Playing Rules of the Rep Division or the best interests of Baseball Ontario. In areas where no Local Association exists, the Affiliated Association may set policy. In the absence of such a policy, The Rep Division shall apply the following policy: The player may play for any team in the Affiliated Association without a release. In areas where there is a Local Association, but the Local Association does not have a team at the particular series, the Affiliated Association may set policy. In the absence of such a policy, The Rep Division shall apply the following policy: The player may play for any team in the Affiliated Association without a release. For the purposes of this rule, minor (e.g., 8U, 10U, 12U, 14U, 16U) is not defined as a separate series from major (e.g., 9U, 11U, 13U, 15U, 18U). If a combined minor/major program is offered by a Local Association, then the Local Association is considered to have a team at the particular series. The player is expected to return to his/her Local Association, unless a released is obtained pursuant to RP1.5. A player in the circumstances of e) or f) may play for a team in another Affiliated Association without a release where such team is the team closest to his/her place of residence. Closest team shall be interpreted as the distance from the player’s home to the relevant teams’ home baseball diamond as the crow flies. Where a Local Association has more than 1 team operating in the same series, the Local Association shall set policy and/or boundaries for such teams. The Local Association shall file a copy of such policy and/or boundaries with the Affiliated Association and Baseball Ontario. [TOP] RP1.4 Players Changing Residence Baseball Ontario recognizes that families move. As set out in RP1.3b), players must establish residence prior to June 30th of the playing season. In split family situations (i.e., the parents are divorced or separated), players 18 years of age and younger shall be deemed to reside where the parent(s) having custody rights reside. Baseball Ontario does not condone moves of convenience or falsified family splits designed to bypass the release process. Baseball Ontario, when considering the legitimacy of the family move or family split, may have regard to a number of factor(s), including, but not limited to the following: a statement from the parent(s) outlining that they actually live at the stated address; a copy of a rental agreement or proof of ownership of the residence; a driver’s license with the stated address; the name and address of the school the player attends; a separation agreement or divorce decree outlining the custody and access arrangements of the player; and/or Canada Revenue Agency Marital Status Change Form – RC 65 [TOP] RP1.5 Players Changing Teams, Releases, and Transfers Players are only allowed to be registered with one team at any time. Players registered with one team must be properly released before registering with a second team. This applies to all series. A player’s playing rights are held by: (i) the Local Association(s) where they reside as set forth in RP1.3 and RP1.4; and (ii) the Local Association where they played the previous season if the player obtained all necessary releases under this section RP1.5. At the Affiliated Association level, a player’s playing rights are held by the Affiliated Association(s) where they reside as set forth RP1.3 and RP1.4. Before a player may participate in a practice, game, tryout, evaluation, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights, the player must obtain the appropriate authorization as specified below. Where a player wishes to participate in another team’s out-of-province game or tournament and is registered with the Local Association that holds their playing rights, then permission from the Local Association and the Affiliated Association are required. Where a player desires to register with a Direct Entry or Local Association that does not hold their playing rights, the player must obtain: a release from each Local Association that hold their playing rights as set out in RP1.5 b); and a release from the Affiliated Association where the player resides, if they desire to register with a Direct Entry or Local Association outside of the Affiliated Association where they reside. Note: The request for a release must be in writing and the granting or refusal of a release shall be in writing authorized by the current management of the Direct Entry, Local Association or Affiliated Association involved. The foregoing does not apply at AAAA, 22U, or Senior where no release is required, and a player may sign with a team that does not hold their playing rights without a release as long as there is no equipment, uniform or financial obligation owing to their previous team. Where a player desires to participate in a tryout, evaluation, practice, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights, the player must obtain the releases required by RP1.5e). Release requests must be responded to and adjudicated by the Local Association or Affiliated Association, as the case may be, within 14 days of receipt. The Local Association or Affiliated Association may extend this due date by an additional 14 days should more information be required and must do so in writing to the person requesting the release. Should the request not be responded to within these parameters, the release will be automatically denied. Where a Local Association or Direct Entry grants a player a release, such release is valid and remains in full force and effect so long as the player continues to play for the Local Association or Direct Entry with which the player registered on the basis of having been granted such release. All releases must be in writing and cannot be conditional. Any player, for whom a Local Association is not prepared to offer a spot on one of the Local Association’s OBA teams, is deemed to be released. The Local Association is required to provide a written release upon request by the player. Any player, who has not been played by his/her team in 3 or more consecutive games, has the right to appeal to the OBA Technical Committee should the player’s team refuse to release the player. Releases shall not be valid until filed with the OBA Office. [TOP] RP1.6 Release Appeals Where a player has been denied a release under RP1.5 by a Local Association, the player has the right to appeal in writing the Local Association’s refusal to the Affiliated Association. The Affiliated Association will make a decision to grant or deny the release. The Affiliated Association’s decision is to be in writing. Either the player or the Local Association may appeal the Affiliated Association’s decision to the OBA Technical Committee. No appeal may be made until the Affiliated Association has made its decision. The appeal must include all evidence and correspondence submitted to the Local Association and/or the Affiliated Association and copies of all decisions made by the Local Association and Affiliated Association. The OBA Technical Committee will establish the time and place of the appeal hearing, which will be held within 30 days of receiving the written appeal and all documents required in RP1.6 c). The player (and/or his/her parent(s) or guardian(s)) and the Direct Entry or Local Association and/or Affiliated Association must be present in person at the hearing to provide evidence in support of their position. Failure to appear may result in a default decision. The decision of the OBA Technical Committee shall be final. [TOP] RP1.7 Offenses Where a player participates in a practice, game, tryout, evaluation, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights without the authorization(s) required by RP1.5, the player is subject to suspension by OBA. Any Head Coach, Assistant Coach or manager who allows a player to participate in any practice, game, tryout, evaluation, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights without the authorization(s) required by RP1.5, is subject to suspension by OBA. No player, Head Coach, Assistant Coach or manager may approach any player for whom their Local Association or Direct Entry does not hold the playing rights with the intention of securing said player for their Local Association or Direct Entry. Violation of this rule will leave the player, coach, or manager subject to suspension by OBA. Where a director, officer, employee or other member of a Local Association or Direct Entry (who is not a player, Head Coach, Assistant Coach or manager) approaches any player for whom their Local Association or Direct Entry does not hold the playing rights with the intention of securing said player for their Local Association or Direct Entry, then such violation will leave the Local Association or Direct Entry open to a fine of up to $1000. In addition to or in place of any suspension authorized by this section RP1.7, any person or organization violating any of the provisions of section RP1.7 may be subject to fine or other sanction as determined appropriate by the Technical Committee. [TOP] RP1.8 Advancing to a Higher Series A player registered with a team in any series 22U or below shall be allowed to advance to a higher series of their own Local Association without affecting their status in the series and classification of the team with which they are registered. Any player, who advances, must play with their original team when the team they advance to and the team they are registered with have games on the same date. Where the original (rostered) team is competing in a weekend tournament, the player shall not be eligible to be used as a call up to the team they are advancing to until their original (rostered) team is eliminated from competition in their tournament. (Note: Any team knowingly postponing a game so that a player may advance will be suspended from the Rep Provincial Championships.) (Local Association shall be interpreted as the Local Association with which the player is registered.) In advancing under RP1.8 a), a player may only advance as follows: A 9U (including 8U) player may only advance to 11U. A 11U (including 10U) may advance only to 13U. A 13U (including 12U) may advance only to 15U. A 15U(including 14U) may advance only to 18U. Players who turn 16 years of age in the calendar year may only advance to 22U. For the 15U and 18U National Qualifiers only, OBA teams may call players across from other teams within their Local Association at the same age category, so long as that team is not also playing in the National Qualifier (e.g., an 18U team may call a player to their National Qualifier team from their 17U team (i.e. an 18U team of all 17U aged players), or their 18U A team). For greater clarity, a player may only play for one team in any one National Qualifier tournament. Where a Local Association has more than one team at a series, players that are on the lower classified team may be “called up” to the higher classified team. Where a Local Association has both a Select Division team(s) and a Rep team(s) at a series, players from the Select Division team(s) may be called up to the Rep team(s), provided the players comply with the residency rules and are the proper age. Where a player of a younger age is rostered with a team at an older age category (e.g., A 13U aged player is rostered on a 15U roster), such player shall be considered as a player of the older series at which he/she is rostered despite being of younger age and shall not be eligible to play with the younger team. Where a team wishes to use a call up player from a team that has been eliminated from any further competition during a tournament or a Rep Provincial Championship tournament or a Rep National Qulaifier tournament being held the same weekend, must have either the pitch count records or copies of the official game sheets for all games involving the call-up player. Any player, for whom the pitching eligibility cannot be confirmed, shall not be eligible to pitch. Once discovered, the penalty for violating any provision of RP1.8 shall be forfeiture by the team to which the player advances. The Series Chair shall determine what other appropriate steps will be taken in the circumstances. The Series Chair's decision is final and is not appealable. [TOP] RP1.9 Player Registration Each year, Local Associations will register their players and teams with the OBA through an online registration platform in accordance with policies and procedures determined by the OBA. The OBA will publish such policies and procedures on its website. By virtue of submitting a roster, the Local Association accepts responsibility for the authenticity of the information contained on the roster. The process may commence by a date determined by Rep Council which shall be no later than October 1st annually. Teams must submit a preliminary roster to the OBA Office through the OBA online registration platform no later than April 28th annually or five business days prior to the team's first game, whichever is later. Any team playing without having submitted a roster after May 1st shall be automatically suspended until such time as the roster is submitted. Rosters may be edited by Local Associations until May 31st. No changes to players on a roster may be made after May 31st without the consent of the OBA Rep Commissioner. At 19U Women’s, Senior Women’s, and Senior, submitted rosters may be edited by Local Associations until June 30th. At 19U Women’s, Senior Women’s, and Senior, no changes to players on a roster may be made after June 30th without the consent of the OBA Rep Registrar. At a minimum, the information to be provided through such online registration system will include: Name Date of birth Gender Address of residence (including postal code) Telephone number, and Valid email address Affiliated Associations and the OBA may review submitted rosters to ensure compliance with requirements of player registration and coach certification. Affiliated Associations and the OBA may remove players and coaches not meeting requirements from rosters. Teams with rosters not meeting the requirements of player registration and coach certification are not eligible to participate in Rep Provincial Championships and/or Rep National Qualifier Tournaments. No player may participate in a Rep tournament or Rep Provincial Championship without their name on a team roster. If a player, who is not officially on the team roster or on the roster of a team from which the team may call up players, participates in a game, once it is discovered, the Head Coach will receive a 10-game suspension. The suspension is not appealable. Subject to Baseball Canada eligibility rules, Senior teams attending Baseball Canada Men’s Nationals may add Intercounty Baseball League players as call-ups to their roster for Nationals. [TOP] RP1.10 Team Number A team must have 9 signed players and may have a maximum of 25 players who must be signed to a team roster of the series of the team entered. At 22U and Senior, the maximum roster size is 30 players. A maximum of 25 players can appear on a line-up card for any one game. Each team must have at least 1 manager, and/or 1 or more coaches, signed to the roster clearly marked “Manager” or “Coach” and who shall be responsible for that team. [TOP] RP1.11 Professional Players Registration Any player signed by a professional baseball club of Major League Baseball (MLB) or Minor League Baseball (MiLB), who has received his/her unconditional release or has been placed on the Voluntary Retired List by said professional club before June 30th of the playing season shall become eligible for reinstatement to play in the OBA immediately when he/she returns to his/her home city or town, or to the team he/she left to turn professional or he/she may play with any other team providing he/she qualifies under the residence rule. All applications for reinstatement must be accompanied by a letter from the Office of the President of the MiLB or a copy of the release from the applicable professional organization. Any player signed by an independent professional league or club who has received his/her unconditional release or has been placed on the voluntary retired list by said independent professional club who provides a written statement advising that they no longer play in that league before June 30th of the playing season shall become eligible for reinstatement to play in the OBA immediately when he/she returns to his/her home city or town, or to the team he/she left to turn professional or he/she may play with any other team providing he/she qualifies under the residence rule. A player shall be considered to have turned professional when he/she plays his/her first game, either exhibition or league, with a professional club. The release, voluntary retirement confirmation or written statement must accompany any application for reinstatement. [TOP] RP1.12 Eligibility Players are not eligible to register with the OBA for the then current playing season if they have registered with a non-recognized baseball program operating in the Province of Ontario for any part of such playing season. Players registered with the OBA for the then current playing season who register with a non-recognized baseball program shall be subject to suspension in accordance with section RP7.1. Individual players may participate with a non-recognized program outside of the province if they secure letters of permission from: (1) Rep Head Coach, (2) Local Association Executive, and (3) Affiliate Association Executive; allowing them to play with a specified team or organization. (All letters and information shall be forwarded to the OBA through the Affiliated Association). A player participating with a non-recognized program outside of the province without these approvals shall be automatically suspended indefinitely. Any further penalty shall be determined by the OBA Rep Council. Head Coaches, Assistant Coaches, managers, Rep Council members, Local Association and Affiliated Association Board Members shall immediately disqualify themselves for participation within OBA's Rep Division, its Affiliated Associations and Local Associations, if they are directly involved in any way with a non-recognized baseball program within the Province of Ontario. OBA-carded umpires are not permitted to umpire non-recognized baseball program games. For clarity, umpires are always permitted to umpire any game that involves at least one OBA program. Umpires who violate this rule are subject to immediate and indefinite suspension, pending review. Teams from non-recognized programs may not enter an OBA-sanctioned tournament. For the purpose of this section, the following terms shall have the following meanings: “non-recognized baseball program” shall mean any baseball program other than (1) a baseball program operated exclusively by the OBA, its Affiliated Associations, and its Local Associations, (2) a high school, college or university baseball program, (3) a baseball program operated exclusively by an associated member of the OBA, (4) a baseball program recognized by the Rep Council, or (5) a baseball program operated by an HP Independent Program in accordance with Section RP2.13 playing season” shall mean the period from April 15 through to the end of the Labour Day weekend. “registered” includes any form of signing or registration process and any player participating on a regular basis with a non-recognized baseball program shall be deemed to have been registered with such program. A player participating in another recognized program, including an HP Independent Program, shall have the right to sign only with: the Local Association in whose territory the player lives, or the Local Association with which the player was last rostered. In the case of a player participating with an HP Independent Program, in addition to the above, the Rep team with which the player signs must be classified at AA or below. Despite Section RP1.8 d. and e., a player with an HP Independent Program rostered on a Rep team AA or below may not be used as a call up to a Rep AAA team or an HP-OBA team. To be eligible to participate in a Rep Play-off, a player participating with an HP Independent Program must have participated in 60% of the team’s league games. If they have not, then the player is ineligible to participate in the Rep Play-off. OBA will enforce this rule on the basis of a challenge to a player’s eligibility and not proactively. If there is a challenge, the team will need to provide evidence of the player’s eligibility. Teams may apply to the Rep Commissioner for an exemption from the 60% requirement for extenuating circumstances (e.g. injury). The Rep Commissioner, in consultation with the Rep Deputy Commissioner, shall rule on the exemption request. Such determination is final. If the player is not signed by such Local Association, then he/she shall not be entitled to sign with another Local Association. Senior Players are exempt from RP1.12 g) [TOP] RP1.13 High Performance Programs There shall be two types of High Performance Programs recognized by OBA: HP OBA PROGRAMS (HP-OBA) Requirements: To register as an HP-OBA Program, the program must: be operated by a Local Association or Affiliated Association and roster with OBA through OBA’s approved platform, including paying applicable fees through its Affiliated Association; adhere to the age categories approved by Baseball Canada; ensure all coaches for each team obtain and maintain the minimum coaching standards for their age category for a AAA coach as published by OBA and for nationals as published by Baseball Canada; follow OBA Arm Care rules and use the OBA Pitch Count app in all games throughout the season. Opportunities: HP-OBA Programs may: compete in the Rep National Qualifier Tournament, if applicable; actively promote their try-outs on social media, websites or other public advertisements at any time; at 12U and above, sign players without the need to follow the release requirements of RP1.5 and hold early try-outs, subject to the timelines identified below. Timelines: To take advantage of the no release requirement and early try-out opportunities for the following season, an HP-OBA Program must: declare their intention to register as an HP-OBA Program for the following season by May 1 annually, otherwise the release requirements of RP1.5 would apply; not engage in recruitment or hold try-outs prior to: July 1 in the case of teams operating at 12U or 13U in the following season; or May 1 in the case of teams operating at 14U and above in the following season; complete their try-outs and announce their roster for the following season’s team by the HP-OBA Roster Deadline of: November 1 for teams operating at 12U or 13U in the following season; or The Wednesday after Labour Day for teams operating at 14U and above in the following season. For purposes of these timelines, “recruitment” is defined as any solicitation of prospective players or their parents by a staff member or authorized representative of an OBA-HP Program for the purpose of securing a prospective player’s position at a try-out or on a team operated by the HP-OBA Program. Releases and Playing Rights: Players who wish to sign with an HP-OBA Program for the following season: do not require a release under Section RP1.5 if included on an HP-OBA Program roster submitted by the applicable HP-OBA Roster Deadline; must obtain all releases required by Section RP1.5 if signed after the applicable HP-OBA Roster Deadline. If denied, an appeal may be filed in accordance with Section RP1.6; have their playing rights for non-High Performance Program play remain with the Local Association(s) and Affiliated Association determined in accordance with Section RP1.5. Classification and Competition: HP-OBA Programs: shall be classified AAA; may compete in any league they wish. HP INDEPENDENT PROGRAMS (HP-INDEPENDENT PROGRAM) Registration To register as an HP-Independent Program, the program must: not be part of a Local Association or Affiliated Association; annually declare to be an HP-Independent Program and pay the prescribed fees as determined by the OBA Board of Directors; not register any player 11U or younger on any team operated by such HP-Independent Program during the playing season. Opportunities: HP-Independent Program have players drafted to the team(s) representing Ontario at a Baseball Canada National Championship; have players on the Ontario Youth Team; have access to the OBA Pitch Count app and OBA OnDeck app at no cost; have access to any level of OBA certified umpires; choose to purchase OBA insurance. HP-Independent Programs may not compete in any Rep Play-off or Rep National Qualifier Tourna

searchText: Rep Division Procedures Rep Division Procedures Table of Contents R1.01 GENERAL R1.02 REP DIVISION R1.03 MEMBERSHIP R1.04 DIRECT ENTRY GUARANTEE R2.01 REP COUNCIL STRUCTURE R2.02 DUTIES OF COUNCILLORS R2.03 MEETINGS R2.04 AMENDMENTS R3.01 PROVINCIAL REGISTRATION FEES R4.01 BOUNDARIES R5.01 HOUSEHOLD COUNTS R5.02 CLASSIFICATION (Pilot) R6.01 CHAMPIONSHIP DATES R6.02 CONVENORS R6.03 VISITING TEAM EXPENSES R7.01 SPECIAL PLAYING RULES COMMITTEE R7.02 Senior Advisory Committee Skip to main content Rep Division Procedures – Rep Rule Book COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER Rep Division Procedures Updated for the 2026 Playing Season Table of Contents R1.01 General R1.02 Rep Division R1.03 Membership R1.04 Direct Entry Guarantee R2.01 Rep Council Structure R2.02 Duties of Councillors R2.03 Meetings R2.04 Amendments R3.01 Provincial Registration Fees R4.01 Boundaries R5.01 Household Counts R5.02 Classificiation R6.01 Championship Dates R6.02 Convenors R6.03 Visiting Team Expenses R7.01 Special Playing Rules Committee R7.02 Senior Advisory Committee R1.01 GENERAL In addition to the provisions of the OBA By-Laws and the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules, rep play shall be governed by the provisions of the Procedures and Playing Rules of the Rep Division, which may not include any provision that conflicts with the OBA By-Laws or the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules. To the extent that there is any conflict between the OBA By-Laws, the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and/or the Rep Procedures and Playing Rules, the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules shall prevail. The Rep Procedures and Playing Rules as shown in this document are intended as a general guide and cannot specifically cover each situation that may arise. Therefore, the Commissioner in regular or special meetings, with a quorum of the Council, will have the authority to interpret and decide to the best of their judgement with regard to all the circumstances or any specific case, any matter pertaining to any and all clauses contained herein. Their decision shall be final. The Rep Division acknowledges that the OBA Board of Directors is empowered to impose rule changes in the best interests of baseball as per paragraph A1.01(e) of the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules. Any Rep member association and/or officers of same, which fails to uphold the rulings and enforce the decisions of the OBA will leave said Rep Member Association, and/or officers open to suspension by the OBA. [TOP] R1.02 REP DIVISION The Rep Division shall regulate competition throughout the province at the Rep level, including provincial championships and championships to determine provincial representatives at national championships. The objects of the OBA Rep Division shall be in order of priority: To foster and improve baseball at all levels in the Province of Ontario. To protect and promote the mutual interests of the members. To institute and regulate competition for the Ontario championships. To institute and regulate competition for determining the Ontario representative(s) in the national or Canadian play‑offs. [TOP] R1.03 MEMBERSHIP Rep Members shall consist of such Affiliated Associations, Local Associations, and Direct Entries, which shall agree to abide by and comply with the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and the Rep Procedures and Playing Rules. Members of the Rep Council, both those elected and appointed, Life Members, Honorary Members, Past Commissioners and Past Presidents shall also be Rep Members. The Rep Council shall have the power to accept individual teams where they are unable to secure grouping in any Affiliated Association. For purposes of the Rep Procedures and Playing Rules, the term “Direct Entry” shall mean a team affiliating directly with the Rep Division and not through an Affiliated Association. Note: A baseball association, which is a direct member of the Rep Division, will be known as an “Affiliated Association” and a baseball association, which is a direct member of an Affiliated Association, and an indirect member of the Rep Division, shall be known as a “Local Association”. Teams Eligible: To be eligible for Rep Provincial Championships, teams must represent a municipality or municipalities, or a portion thereof, within the boundaries of the Province of Ontario and must be made up of players who qualify under the residence rule. Players Eligible: To be eligible, all players must qualify under the residence rule. Former professional baseball players who have been re‑instated by the Rep Division in accordance with [Rep Rule RP1.8] and athletes who are classified as professionals in other sports are eligible. Every Affiliated Association desirous of joining the Rep Division shall send to the OBA Office a completed application for membership, a list of its officers, and a copy of its by-laws. All teams of Local Associations of any classification desirous of entering the Rep Provincial Championships must affiliate through an Affiliated Association in their area or directly with the Rep Division where there is no Affiliated Association in their area. Teams of Local Associations must register with the Rep Division through the Affiliated Association in which territory they play their home games (“Home Affiliate”). Teams may be released to participate in a league schedule of another Affiliated Association (“League Affiliate”) but will be registered with and classified by the Home Affiliate. Released teams and their players will be subject to the rules and regulations of the League Affiliate. Released teams will be subject to the player release rules as set out by the Home Affiliate. Team releases must be in writing and there will be no charge for the release of any such team. No participant may be disqualified from participating in the Rep Division for reason of race, religion, gender, identity, or orientation. [TOP] R1.04 DIRECT ENTRY GUARANTEE Each Direct Entry must pay a guaranteed deposit of $500.00 to be returned only if the Direct Entry completes its obligations as stated in the OBA By‑Laws and the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules. [TOP] R2.01 REP COUNCIL STRUCTURE The operations of the Rep Division shall be handled by the Rep Council. The members of the Rep Council shall be the Rep Commissioner, Rep Deputy Commissioner, the Rep Immediate Past Commissioner, 6 Rep Councillors, up to 3 Councillors appointed by the Rep Commissioner to Rep Council, all Life Members, Honorary Members, Past Commissioners and 1 appointed Rep Council Member from each Affiliated Association to be appointed by that Affiliated Association. The Rep Operational Committee shall be appointed by the Rep Commissioner from members of the Rep Council and shall have authority to conduct the day-to-day business of the the Rep Division between Council meetings. Such business shall be reported to the Rep Council at the next meeting of the Council. The members of the Rep Operational Committee shall consist of (1) the Rep Commissioner, (2) the Rep Vice Commissioner, and (3) at least two, but as many other members of Rep Council as the Rep Commissioner considers appropriate. The President and Vice President of the OBA are considered ex-officio members of the Rep Operational Committee. For a period of two years following the election of a new Rep Commissioner, the Immediate Past Commissioner will be appointed as an additional member of the Rep Operational Committee. After that two-year period, the Immediate may or may not be one of the persons appointed to the Rep Operational Committee pursuant to R1.03(d). The Rep Commissioner may appoint, from time to time, 1 or more committees of the Rep Council as required to consider such matters as may be dealt with by the Council and generally act on behalf of the Rep Council between meetings of the Rep Council. Such committee(s) shall be comprised of 1 or more members of the Rep Council as the Rep Commissioner may deem fit. The Rep Council shall meet once a month during the season when business warrants. 7 members shall form a quorum for meetings of the Rep Council. Any member of the Rep Council (excluding Life Members, Honorary Members, Past Presidents and Rep Past Commissioners) who fails to attend 3 consecutive meetings, without reasons that in the opinion of the Rep Council are satisfactory, shall have automatically tendered his/her resignation. After 2 consecutive misses, this detail shall be brought to his/her attention by a letter from the OBA Office. Should the third consecutive miss occur, except for a member appointed by an Affiliated Association, the Rep Council will appoint a replacement. If the member was an appointee from an Affiliated Association, that Affiliated Association shall be requested to appoint the replacement and must notify the OBA Office of such appointment within 3 weeks. The President and Vice President of the OBA are deemed to be voting ex-officio members of Rep Council. [TOP] R2.02 DUTIES OF COUNCILLORS The Rep Council shall be responsible for the overall direction of the Rep Division. The Rep Council shall approve all policies of the Rep Division. OBA Past Presidents, who served in Office prior to 2017, will have all the rights, privileges, and responsibilities of a Rep Past Commissioner. [TOP] R2.03 MEETINGS The Rep Annual Division Meeting (which term shall include a Rep Annual and Special Division Meeting) shall be held in conjunction with the Select Annual Division Meeting. No delegate shall be permitted to speak or vote at a Rep Annual Division Meeting or Rep Special Division Meeting unless he/she has filed with the Secretary, a certificate of his/her appointment, signed by the Secretary of the Affiliated Association or Direct Entry which he/she represents. Annual member’s fees for the then current period October 1st to September 30th must be paid by an Affiliated Association or Direct Entry before that Affiliated Association’s or Direct Entry’s delegate(s) shall have a voice or vote at Annual Meetings or Special Meetings of the Rep Division. Special Meetings: The Rep Commissioner may call, at their discretion, a Special General Meeting of the Rep Division (Special Division Meeting) at any time but must do so at the request of 3 Affiliated Associations. At all Rep Special Division Meetings, 8 delegates shall form a quorum. 21 days’ notice of such meeting must be given and the reason for such meeting. Affiliated Associations must operate the previous year under the jurisdiction of the OBA before having the right of voice at a Rep Annual Division Meeting or Special Division Meeting. At a Rep Annual Division Meeting or Special Division Meeting, each Affiliated Association shall be entitled to the following votes: Affiliated Associations with more than 24 teams 4 votes Affiliated Associations with 15 to 24 teams 3 votes Affiliated Associations with 6 to 14 teams 2 votes Affiliated Associations with 1 to 5 teams 1 vote No delegate from an Affiliated Association in arrears to the OBA in any way, shall be permitted to vote at a Rep Annual Division Meeting providing notice of such arrears has been given in writing to the Affiliated Association a month prior to the Rep Annual Division Meeting. A delegate may only vote as a representative of the Affiliated Association through which he/she is registered. Electing Officers and Councillors The Rep Commissioner will be elected at the OBA Annual Meeting in accordance with the OBA By-laws. Nominations for Rep Deputy Commissioner and Rep Councillors shall be submitted annually to the OBA Office by a deadline established by the Board of Directors. Any Rep Member in good standing may submit a nomination. OBA shall, at the Rep Annual Division Meeting, provide delegates with a ballot for the election of Rep Deputy Commissioner. The candidate receiving the most votes will be declared elected. OBA shall at the Rep Annual Division Meeting provide delegates with a ballot for election of 6 Rep Councillors. All eligible voting delegates must mark their ballots for 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6 of the candidates nominated and where such is not done, the ballot shall be considered spoiled and not counted. The 6 candidates receiving the most votes shall be declared elected. Each person nominated for Rep Deputy Commissioner or Rep Councillor shall be given a maximum of two minutes to speak about their candidacy. Nominees may decline to speak. [TOP] R2.04 AMENDMENTS Amendments to the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules An amendment to the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules may only be made at a Rep Annual Division Meeting or Rep Special Division Meeting and must be approved by a majority of the votes cast in respect of such amendments in order to carry. Notice of any proposed amendment must be filed with the OBA Office in writing by September 25th prior to the Rep Annual Meeting and the OBA Office shall then electronically forward a copy to each Affiliated Association no later than November first, before the Rep Annual Meeting. Amendments passed and carried shall become effective at the conclusion of the Rep Annual Meeting at which they are passed. An amendment may only be proposed by the Rep Council or by Local Association or Affiliated Association in good standing. At a Rep Annual Division Meeting, the Rep Council may propose a resolution for an experiment that differs from the Rep Procedures and Playing Rules. This experiment may be put into effect as an experiment for no more than 2 years. In order to be adopted, the resolution must be approved by 2/3 of the votes cast in respect of the resolution and would be subject to annual review by the Rep Council. Any references in the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules that are contrary to the experiment are suspended while the experiment is in progress. Once the time period for the experiment has expired, unless an amendment to the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules is adopted with respect to the subject matter of the experiment, the resolution shall be of no further force and effect and the suspension of any references in the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules shall be removed. Rules of Order of Rep Annual Division Meeting or Rep Special Division Meeting: The following are the regulations of the government of the Rep Division during the time of business, and the business shall be disposed of in the following order: No motion shall be received unless properly moved and seconded. Said motion shall not be open for discussion until so stated by the presiding officer. Except for motions requiring a special majority either pursuant to the Rep Division Procedures and Playing Rules, all motions shall be decided by a majority of votes cast. When the presiding officer sees fit to exercise their right to vote on any question before the meeting, they shall, from the chair, be at liberty to explain their vote, and any question on which there is an equality of votes in consequence of the presiding Councillor’s vote, shall be deemed to be negative. No delegate shall speak twice on the same motion without the permission of the chair, unless in explanation, or the mover in reply. A delegate desiring to speak or submit a motion shall rise and remain standing until recognized by the chair. In speaking, a delegate shall respectfully address the chair and shall confine their remarks to the motion under debate. The delegate shall not be interrupted unless upon a point of order. Non-delegates may be permitted to speak at the discretion of the chair. No amendment to a motion shall be in order after an amendment to the amendment. When a motion is under debate, no motion shall be entertained except to lay on the table or amend and these motions shall take precedence in the order named. An amendment, which entirely changes the subject of the original motion, shall not be entertained as an amendment, or substituted for the motion under debate. After the motion has been stated by the presiding officer it becomes the property of the Rep Division but may be withdrawn at any time prior to amendment, unless objected to by a delegate. There shall be no debate upon any question after it has been put by the presiding Councillor. When a vote is called, it shall be taken by each delegate holding up their right hand. The presiding officer shall declare the vote on all questions, and should their declaration be disputed, they may require the delegates voting “Yea” or “Nay” to rise and stand until the number voting for the affirmative and the negative be counted by the presiding Councillor. [TOP] R3.01 PROVINCIAL REGISTRATION FEES A Provincial Registration Fee as determined by the OBA Board of Management and Rep Council, will be paid by the Affiliated Association or Direct Entry, as the case may be, to the OBA for each of its rep teams. The Provincial Registration Fee must be paid by July 8th to the OBA Office. Failure to pay the Provincial Registration Fee shall result in the team(s) not being classified. [TOP] R4.01 BOUNDARIES The status of all Affiliated Associations is defined as territorial rights, as set forth in Appendix B. The boundaries of each have been approved and set by the Rep Council. Any new baseball associations, which do not conflict with any established Affiliated Association, which desire to affiliate with the Rep Division, must first be approved by the Rep Council. Two Local Associations in different Affiliated Associations cannot amalgamate across Affiliated Association boundaries without the written permission of both Affiliated Associations and the approval of OBA. All applications by Direct Entries for direct entry must be approved by the Rep Council before being accepted. The Rep Council shall have the power to mediate boundary disputes and make changes to Affiliated Association boundaries where such changes are deemed to be in the best interests of baseball. [TOP] R5.01 HOUSEHOLD COUNTS The OBA shall offer for annual competition the following Rep Provincial Championships: Category 30,001+ Households Less than 30,001 8U AAA, AA, A B 9U AAA, AA, A B, C, D 10U AAA, AA, A B 11U AAA, AA, A B, C, D 12U AAA, AA, A B 13U AAA, AA, A B, C, D 14U AAA, AA, A B 15U AAA, AA, A B, C, D 16U AAA, AA, A B 18U AAA, AA, A B, C Category All Households 16U Girls AAA 22U AAA, AA, A, B Women AAA Senior AAA, AA, A, B, C Household numbers are used only to determine categories teams are eligible to participate in. Teams may request higher classification if so desired. A team at a lower household category may be classified at the classification of a larger household category at its request. For purposes of classification, the number of households will be based on Canada Post data. Each Affiliated Association must define which postal codes are contained within their Local Associations’ boundaries. Urban centres will use the FSA (first three digits of the postal code) and rural centres will use all 6 digits of the postal code. The information must be filed with the OBA Office by April 1, 2012, and updated as necessary every three years thereafter. If an Affiliated Association fails to comply with this requirement, the Competition Committee will decide whether or not the Affiliated Association’s teams will be eligible to be classified, and if so, will determine the classification of the teams. The Competition Committee’s decision is final and may not be appealed. The number of households added to the household count of a team by an import player(s) will be determined as follows: Each urban postal code will add the highest count associated with the LCW, LB, SS, RR, or GD (as defined by Canada Post) for the FSA in which the players reside. Each rural postal code will add the highest count associated with the LCW, LB, SS, RR or GD (as defined by Canada Post) for the postal code in which the players reside. The import player household count adder will be calculated as the average of the household counts determined in the prior two steps. (Note: this will determine a single number that will be used as an adder for each player residing outside of the Local Association postal code(s), regardless of whether they reside in an urban or rural postal code. This number will be revised every 3 years by March 31st, in the same year that Local Association postal codes are eligible for changing). The import player household count adder will be recalculated with the update of the Canada Post information on which it is based. Canada post data will be updated annually by March 31st. Where two or more Local Associations within the same Affiliated Association combine their players to field a single team, they may apply to have the combined household counts considered as the base amount for the Local Association registering the team, rather than applying the import adder count for each player from the other Local Association(s) involved. Application must be made to the Classification Committee by June 1st. The addition of the household counts from the neighbouring Local Association(s) must be applied to all teams classified by the designated Local Association. If any Local Association feels it has a circumstance that does not fall under the provisions of R4.01a) to d), it has the right to appeal to the Rep Division. [TOP] R5.02 CLASSIFICATION (Pilot) Teams must submit their Classification to the OBA Office on or before April 28th annually, or 5 business days prior to the team’s first game of the season, whichever is later. The method of classification shall be adding the classification to their roster ID in the OBA-approved rostering system (ie: 13U AAA). Failure to submit such information by the deadline shall result in the applicable team(s) not being permitted to participate in OBA Championships. Once a team classifies for the season, they will not be permitted to lower their classification, however they may choose to classify higher if they wish. The Rep Operations Committee shall determine the legitimacy of all teams' classifications. Affiliated Associations must declare their teams’ intention to participate in the OBA Championship that they classified in at the start of the season, no later than June 30th. The OBA Rep Commissioner shall appoint a Competition Committee to review the number of teams desirous to play in the OBA Championship. The Competition Committee will meet on the weekend following the second Monday in July to review and ensure that teams are entered for the classification that they submitted at the start of the season. Teams may be reclassified to a higher classification by the Competition Committee at the request of the local and/or affiliated association if deemed appropriate The Competition Committee shall present the final classifications and recommendations for determining additional Play-Off spots to the Rep Council at the July Rep Council meeting. Teams not permitted to classify by an Affiliated Association Affiliated Associations do not have the authority to prohibit a rostered Local Association team from participating in the Provincial Championships except for disciplinary purposes. Disciplinary decisions to remove a team from competing for a Provincial Championship are appealable to the Commissioner of Rep Baseball via the Commissioner’s Appeal process. Where required for classification purposes, the Affiliated Association must submit to the Classification Committee (on a form approved by the Rep Council) the schedule and results of the teams in any league play, the won-lost-tied record, and any tournament results that the teams may have participated in. Failure to comply with these rules will result in the team not being permitted to classify. Once classifications have been approved by the Rep Council, no further changes will be allowed. In any classification where less than 4 teams classify, no Rep Play-off will be held, and those teams which would have been classified at that level shall have the option of moving up one classification to participate in a Rep Play-off. Where they elect not to move up, they will not be classified. Except for Rep Provincial Championships for 8U, 9U and Female Baseball, the number of teams allowed to participate at each classification shall be one per Affiliated Association plus the tournament host. In any series where the number of Affiliated Associations participating plus the tournament host is less than 12, the number of teams which will be allowed to participate would be up to 12 including the tournament host. The Rep Council shall determine the manner of selection of the additional teams to make up the 12-team tournament. If there are less than 12 teams classified at a classification, all teams shall be permitted to participate. At 8U, 9U and Female Baseball, the number of teams allowed to participate at each classification shall be one per Affiliated Association plus the tournament host. In any series where the number of Affiliated Associations participating plus the host is less than 16 teams, the number of teams which will be allowed to participate would be up to 16 including the tournament host. The Rep Council shall determine the manner of the selection of the additional teams to make up the 16-team tournament. If there are less than 16 teams classified at a classification, all teams may be permitted to participate. At Senior the following limits apply to the number of teams for each Rep Play-off Tournament: Senior AAA shall be unlimited; and Senior AA and below shall be limited to 12 teams, with no more than 2 teams coming from any one Affiliated Association. Additional teams may be added from an Affiliated Association to bring the tournament to a maximum of 12 teams including the host. Except at Senior AAA and Senior AA, the champions in any category shall be classified in the next high category the following year. The Rep Council shall decide annually the method by which the winner of each Rep Play-off shall be declared. This method shall consist of one of the following: home and home style Rep Provincial Chamionships; or a double loss knock-out tournament with a minimum of 6 teams scheduled to be held no later than the Labour Day weekend; or a round robin tournament; or a combination. No player may participate with more than 1 team in any 1 series. Any team classified at the Rep classification meeting must participate in all its Rep Provincial Championships. If a team fails to fulfil its commitment a fee of 1.5 times the entry fee for that tournament will be assessed. Local Associations and Affiliated Associations shall be responsible for ensuring payment of such fines assessed against their teams. In order to be classified, a team must have played and reported a minimum of 5 games against other OBA Rep rostered teams and those games must be documented in the Pitch Count app. Except at 18U AAA, at each age category where the winner of the National Qualifier Tournament is not deemed a Rep Play-off Champion, the winner of the National Qualifier Tournament shall be granted an automatic entry into the Rep Play-off Tournament for which teams entering the National Qualifier Tournament are classified. The National Qualifier Tournament winners will not be considered the representative of the Affiliated Association to which it belongs, and the Affiliated Association shall be entitled to send another of its teams as its representative. National Qualifier Tournament winners must declare their intentions to the OBA Office and the Rep Play-off Tournament host by August 15. For 8U, 9U and Girls Baseball, the OBA Play-off Tournament format shall be at the discretion of the Rep Council each year. Where several municipalities or Local Associations of C or lower classifications desire to compete in open tournaments at the A, AA, and AAA level, they may choose to register an Affiliated Association approved team for that purpose. Such teams shall not be eligible for Rep Provincial Championships and National Qualifier Tournaments, and players on such a team are obliged to honour commitments to their Local Association first. [TOP] R6.01 CHAMPIONSHIP DATES Each Affiliated Association in order to participate in the Rep Provincial Championships must be prepared to enter the Rep Provincial Championships on the dates as set by the Rep Council (for all series) but in no case shall that date be later than the fourth Saturday in August. Note: Labour Day weekend (except in Senior, 22U, 18U, 9U, 8U and Girl’s Baseball) is the recommended dates for Tournament style Rep Provincial Championships. No Rep Play‑off may be ordered after October 5th. The Series Chair has the power to order a tournament to be held to declare a winner, if deemed necessary. Where exceptional circumstances arise in Affiliated Associations, only the Rep Council or its committee(s) are empowered to extend the date of declaring a winner. The request for extension must be at the OBA Office at least 2 weeks prior to the Rep Play‑off date. Girls Baseball Rep Provincial Championships will be scheduled at least 1 week but not more than 2 weeks prior to Labour Day weekend. All 8U and 9U Rep Provincial Championships will be scheduled on a weekend prior to the Labour Day weekend, as determined by Rep Council. All 18U Rep Provincial Championships will be scheduled 2 weeks after the August Civic Holiday weekend. Except at 22U AAA which will be the 22U National Qualifier Tournament, all 22U Rep Provincial Championships will be scheduled 1 week after the August Civic Holiday weekend. Except at Senior AAA which will be the Senior National Qualifier Tournament, all Senior Rep Provincial Championships will be scheduled on a weekend other than Labour Day weekend, as determined by Rep Council. Rep Play-off tournament schedules shall be published at least 2 weeks prior to the start date of the tournament (with the exception of Senior which shall be 3 weeks prior to the start of the tournament) as defined in R7.01 a) through h).
