title: Oba Pitch Count App User Guide
snippet: Skip to main content OBA Pitch Count App User Guide – Arm Care COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER OBA Pitch Count App User Guide Updated for the 2026 baseball season OBA Pitch Count App User Guide The OBA Pitch Count A
searchText: Oba Pitch Count App User Guide OBA Pitch Count App User Guide Skip to main content OBA Pitch Count App User Guide – Arm Care COACHING CENTRE UMPIRE ZONE FEMALE BASEBALL ARM CARE MORE SIGN IN / REGISTER OBA Pitch Count App User Guide Updated for the 2026 baseball season OBA Pitch Count App User Guide The OBA Pitch Count App is provided free for all OBA teams. Only Coaches and rostered staff are permitted access to the full functionality of the app, such as entering games, adding players, and adding pitch counts. Coaches may delegate access to a volunteer to enter pitch counts only. In order for coaches to access the app, you must be on an approved OBA roster with a valid and unique email address and your NCCP number. Two coaches cannot share the same email address in the system. Please email pitchcountapp@baseballontario.com for support. Please note this email address is not monitored 24/7, your email will be responded to as soon as possible. This user guide is intended to provide information on how to use the app and any mention to the pitch count rules are for reference purposes only and should not be considered the actual rule. Please see the Playing Rules for full text of the rules. IMPORTANT NOTE: The Home Team must enter the pitch counts for both teams. Do not enter multiple entries for the same game (ie: one game for the away team and one game for the home team). Both teams' pitch counts must be entered as one game by the home team. Download the App Pitch Count App User Guide Adding a New Game Adding Pitch Counts Live-in-Game Adding Pitch Counts Post-Game Adding a Call-Up Player to a Team Approving or Challenging Pitch Counts Change Uniform Number Checking Pitcher Availability Deleting a Game Deleting Pitch Counts Editing After Approval (Reopen) Edit Game Details Edit Score After Approval Favourite Teams Game Statuses No Opponent Games (Out of Province Tournaments/High School/Ad Hoc) Removing a Pitcher from a Game Registration to Pitch Count App Signing in to the App Statistics Additional How-To Series Troubleshooting Login & Access Issues Pitch Count Support ADDING A NEW GAME From the main screen of Pitch Count, tap your team name under My Teams. Select the Games tab and tap ⊕ ADD GAME Enter the Game Date and Game Time. Select the Visiting Team. Pro Tip: You can search for the correct team by team number, team name, or filter by division). Add the game Location. Tap Save. Remember: Home Team must enter pitch counts for both teams. [TOP] ADDING PITCH COUNTS, LIVE IN-GAME Capturing pitch counts live-in-game is the preferred method of counting pitches. This eliminates the risk of pitchers exceeding pitch counts, using pitchers who are in recovery, and alerts both coaches to potential rule violations during the game. From the Games tab, select the game you wish to count pitches for. Tap . BEGIN PITCH COUNT . Select the pitcher you wish to start the Top of the 1st inning. You may be asked to enter the pitcher's jersey number, enter that here (you can change it later) and tap Update, otherwise Skip. Tap the large blue + sign to add pitches as they are delivered. Pro Tip: All pitches delivered to the batter must be counted as a pitch, this includes balks that are thrown to the batter. A balk that is thrown to a base, or a balk that is not thrown is not counted. The app will display in large numbers how many pitches have been thrown this inning by this pitcher. This will turn amber when the pitcher has 10 pitches left until they hit their daily maximum and red when they hit their daily maximum. Above the pitches thrown this inning, the app displays a countdown of how many pitches this pitcher has remaining today. This will turn amber when the pitcher has 10 pitches left until they hit their daily maximum and red when they hit their daily maximum. You can edit how many pitches have been thrown in the inning by selecting the pencil icon above the pitches remaining today. Tap . Next . to select a relief pitcher, or to move on to the next half-inning. When the game is complete, tap the back button on your device to go back to the main screen for the game. Tap . END GAME AND REQUEST APPROVAL . to finalize the game. Note: When you request approval, the game is locked and requires verification from the visiting team. [TOP] ADDING PITCH COUNTS, POST GAME If you are unable to capture pitch counts live-in-game using the app, pitch counts may be captured on paper and entered in the app post-game. From the Games tab, select the game you wish to count pitches for. Tap . ADD TOTAL PITCH COUNT . Select the first team you wish to add pitches for. Select the pitcher you wish to add pitches for. Enter the total number of pitches thrown by that pitcher. Tap UPDATE Repeat steps 2-6 until all pitches have been entered for both teams. When the game is complete, tap the back button on your device to go back to the main screen for the game. Tap . END GAME AND REQUEST APPROVAL . to finalize the game. Note: When you request approval, the game is locked and requires verification from the visiting team. [TOP] ADDING A CALL-UP PLAYER TO A TEAM From the Roster tab, tap ⊕ ADD PLAYER Search the player's first and last name in the system. Note: The player you wish to add must be on an approved Baseball Ontario roster uploaded into the Pitch Count database already in order to add them to a team. Players matching the name searched will populate in the Search Results screen with the team name that they are a full-time roster player on. Select the correct player from the list. A confirmation pop-up will appear, tap ADD PLAYER if you wish to add this player to your team. [TOP] APPROVING OR CHALLENGING PITCH COUNTS Once the Home Team has submitted a pitch count for approval, the Visiting Team must verify the pitch count is accurate within 5 days of submission. All coaches on the team will receive a notification that a game approval has been requested. Log in to the Pitch Count app. Approval Requests will display at the top of the app, tap the game you wish to verify. Verify that all pitch counts have been entered correctly. Tap . APPROVE PITCH COUNTS . if the pitch counts were entered correctly. Tap . CHALLENGE PITCH COUNTS . if there are corrections that need to be made on the pitch counts. When you challenge the pitch count, you must enter a message to the Home Team coach why you are challenging the pitch count. The game status will change to CHALLENGED and the Home Team can then edit the pitch counts and resubmit for approval. Pro Tip: If a game is not verified, the app will auto-verify the game after 2 days. [TOP] CHANGE UNIFORM NUMBER Log in to the Pitch Count app. Select the team you wish to edit under the MY TEAMS header. Select the ROSTER tab at the top. Android: Tap the ellipsis (three vertical dots) to the right of the player's name on the player card and tap Edit. Enter the new uniform number and tap UPDATE. Apple: Swipe left on the player card that you wish to edit and tap Edit. Enter the uniform number and tap Save. [TOP] CHECKING PITCHER AVAILABILITY From the main screen, select your team under the MY TEAMS header. Select the ROSTER tab. Each player on the roster will have a player card displayed on this tab. The player's jersey number, if entered, is displayed in the circle next to the player's name. The number of pitches thrown on the pitcher's last outing and the date they last threw will appear on this card. If the pitcher has any pitches remaining today, they will also appear on this card. Pitcher's statuses are colour-coded to help identify if a player is able to pitch today: Available - Pitcher is available to pitch today. In Recovery - Pitcher is not available to pitch today. The next available date the pitcher is available to pitch will display on the card. [TOP] DELETING A GAME For a game to be deleted, there can be no pitcher attached to the game, please see Deleting Pitch Counts to remove all pitchers from the game prior to deleting the game. Ensure all pitchers have been removed from the game. From the games screen select the game you wish to delete. Android: Tap the pencil icon in the top right corner of the game screen and select . DELETE . Apple: Tap the Edit button in the top right corner of the game screen and select Remove Game [TOP] DELETING PITCH COUNTS Pitch Counts for pitchers who have pitches in a game can be removed if they have been entered in error. If a game has been entered in error (or in duplicate), this process must be completed for all pitchers before the game can be deleted. Note: This process can only be completed for games that are In Progress, Challenged, or Reopened status. Deleting entire pitch counts for an inning for a pitcher can be done within the game screen. Android: Tap the ellipsis (three vertical dots) next to the pitch count you wish to delete and tap delete. Apple: Swipe left over the player's pitch count you wish to delete and tap . Delete . On the confirmation screen, confirm you want to delete the pitch count for this pitcher. [TOP] EDITING A GAME'S PITCH COUNTS AFTER SUBMITTING FOR APPROVAL (Reopen) A game can be edited after it has been approved if has been discovered that the pitch counts are incorrect. Either team can initiate this process, but only the Home Team can edit the pitch counts once the game has been re-opened. Only games that have been verified can be reopened with this process. To initiate the reopening of the game: From the Games screen, tap the verified game you wish to reopen. Tap . REQUEST TO REOPEN GAME . Enter a message to the opposing coach to request the game to be reopened. Tap Request to Reopen The opposing coach will receive a notification that the game has been requested to be reopened. The opposing coach must then open the app and choose either Reopen Game or Deny Reopen Request. If the game is reopened, the Home Team coach can edit the pitch counts and resubmit for approval. If the reopen request is denied, the game will automatically revert to Verified status. [TOP] EDIT GAME DETAILS You can change the date, time, game type, and location of a game without deleting the pitch counts but only while the game is in a Pending status. In order to change the date, time, or opponent of a game that is already submitted, you must challenge the game and then all pitch counts already entered in for that game must first be deleted. Please see Deleting Pitch Counts to remove all pitchers from the game prior to changing the game. Ensure all pitchers have been removed from the game. From the games screen select the game you wish to edit. Android: Tap the pencil icon in the top right corner of the game screen and select the field you wish to edit (ie: date, time, Home Team, or Away Team). Tap SAVE. Apple: Tap the Edit button in the top right corner of the game screen and select the field you wish to edit (ie: date, time, Home Team, or Away Team). Tap SAVE. [TOP] EDIT SCORE AFTER APPROVAL To edit scores after the game has been submitted for approval, you must wait for the game to be approved (or approve the game if you are the approver) and then you can request for it to be reopened. Request to reopen the game. Enter "Incorrect game score" in the details. The other coach must approve the reopen request. Once reopened, only the Home Team coach can tap . END GAME AND REQUEST APPROVAL . Change the score and defensive innings. Tap . END GAME AND REQUEST APPROVAL . The Away Team must now re-approve the game. [TOP] FAVOURITE TEAMS Adding Favourite Teams to the Pitch Count app will allow you to quickly select teams you play against more frequently or during tournaments. Search for the team using the search button or one of the filter options. Select the team you wish to make a favourite. Tap the Roster tab to verify the players on the team are correct to ensure you have the correct team. Tap the star icon in the top right corner of the app. [TOP] GAME STATUS STATUS DESCRIPTION No Status Games with no status have not yet been started. In Progress Games with In Progress status have been started but have not yet been submitted for approval. Approval Requested Games with Approval Requested Status have been submitted to the opponent for approval but have not yet been challenged or approved. Challenged Game was submitted by the Home Team but the Visiting Coach challenged the pitch count. Home Team must edit and/or re-submit. Requested to Reopen Game was verified but one of the teams requested the game to be reopened and edited. Reopened Request to reopen the game was approved and editing is in progress. Verified Game has been approved and pitch counts verified. [TOP] NO OPPONENT GAMES Out of Province Tournaments, High School Games, and other Ad Hoc Appearances All pitching appearances for OBA rostered pitchers should be captured in the Pitch Count app, including if the player plays on a non-OBA team (ie: High School, IBL, Senior, etc). To do this, the coach of the player's team should create a game against no opponent and enter the pitcher's total pitch count for the game. The final score can be entered as 0-0 and the defensive innings as 1 inning each. Home Team: Your team Away Team: Do not select another team, leave this blank Game Type: Exhibition Game Location: High School, US Tournament, Ad Hoc, etc. For High School or college games, the app will require you to enter a catcher in order to close the game. Select the same pitcher as your catcher. This will generate an rule violation - that is expected. When the game is submitted for approval, it will be auto-verified by the system after 2 days. Similarly, if a 22U or 18U team is playing a Senior team, the 22U/18U team would enter no opponent because Senior teams do not have a pitch count limitation. In this case, enter the final score and defensive innings correctly. Note: This is the only situation where it is permissible for only one team to enter a pitch count. [TOP] PITCH COUNT APP REGISTRATION Only coaches and staff on a current OBA approved roster can access the Pitch Count app's counting tools. Ensure your coaches and staff email addresses are valid and accurate on the OBA roster submission form. Ensure your coaches are listed as either Head Coach or Assistant Coach on the roster. Ensure your coaches have their NCCP number listed. Ensure staff are listed as Pitch Counter. Submit your roster to OBA. Await approval from Affiliated Association. Once approved, your roster will be uploaded into the Pitch Count app and you will receive an email from Baseball Ontario Support with the subject Welcome to Baseball Ontario. Download the app from the Google Play Store or Apple App Store before you tap the Join button. Tap the join button. Create a password for the app. Sign into the app using the login credentials you just created. Pro Tip: Once you have signed in to the app, if you are unable to see My Teams you are likely signed in as a Guest. Sign out and sign back in. [TOP] SIGNING IN TO THE APP Depending on your access, you can sign into the app in one of two ways: Coaches: Sign into the app using the credentials created through the rostering process (see Registration to Pitch Count App). Players/Parents/Guests: Tap . ENTER AS GUEST . on the sign in screen to enter the app as a guest. You can search for your favourite teams and tap the team you want to follow, then tap the star at the top right to save them as a Favourite. Once you have saved at least one team as a favourite, the app will display My Favorites at the top of the app the next time you come back to the app! [TOP] STATISTICS Pitching Statistics are kept for each player on your team. You can view historical stats showing how many games, innings, and pitches each pitcher has thrown in the last 4 days, 1 week, one month, this season, and all time. From the main screen, tap the team you wish to view stats on. Tap the STATS tab. All stats will display on the screen for each pitcher on your roster. [TOP] TROUBLESHOOTING LOGIN & ACCESS ISSUES Only coaches and staff on an approved OBA roster, submitted through the BaseballShift rostering system will be granted access to the Pitch Count app. Please note, it can take up to 72 hours for rosters to be uploaded to the system. Uploads are not completed on weekends. If you have received your invitation for this year, created your password, and you are able to log in but you are unable to add games and/or approve games, please follow these steps: Uninstall the Pitch Count app (this clears the cache from your device). Reinstall the Pitch Count app but do not log back in yet. Tap the Forgot Password link on the main screen of the Pitch Count app to change your password. Once you have changed your password, log back in to the Pitch Count app using your new credentials. If you are still having issues logging in, please email pitchcountapp@baseballontario.com for assistance. Please note that this email address is not monitored 24/7, your email will be responded to as soon as possible. [TOP] HOW DO I SUBMIT A TROUBLE TICKET FOR THE PITCH COUNT APP? If you encounter a bug, have feedback on the app, or have a question for Pitch Count Support, please email pitchcountapp@baseballontario.com BEFORE YOU EMAIL: Be sure to email from the email address associated with your Pitch Count app if possible. Always include your Team ID which can be found in the URL for your BaseballShift roster (see below). If your question is in regard to a specific game, please include the game number. If your question is in regard to a specific player or coach, please include their full name. Please note that this email address is not monitored 24/7, your email will be responded to as soon as possible. [TOP] Last Updated: 03-June-2024 SearchMember DirectoryEventsEmail Support Copyright © 2026 New Start Mobile, LLC https://ondeck.baseballontario.com/page/784/arm-care/16451/oba-pitch-count-app-user-guide
headings: OBA Pitch Count App User Guide

Guidelines for Affiliated Associations Appendix B - Affiliated Associations Appendix C - National Championship Player Drafts RP1.1 Age and Category To be eligible to play in a particular age category, a player must not have a birthday which makes them older than the number in the series name. ie: A player in 9U (9 and Under) must not turn 10 dururing the current calendar year. CATEGORY/SERIES AGE OF PLAYER AS OF DECEMBER 31st OF THE CALENDAR YEAR 8U Not older than 8 years of age but not younger than 6 as of December 31st 9U Not older than 9 years of age 10U Not older than 10 years of age 11U Not older than 11 years of age 12U Not older than 12 years of age 13U/13U Girls Not older than 13 years of age 14U Not older than 14 years of age 15U Not older than 15 years of age 16U/16U Girls Not older than 16 years of age 18U Not older than 18 years of age 22U Not older than 22 years of age Senior/Women’s Unlimited but not younger than 17 as of December 31st [TOP] RP1.2 Birth Records All players 21 years of age and under must produce a birth record the first time they register with OBA. Only birth certificates, passports, citizenship cards or driver’s licenses will be accepted as birth records. In the case of Affiliated Associations having their own registrar and having the approval of the OBA, it is not necessary to send birth records to the OBA Office if such birth records are provided to the Affiliated Association’s registrar and will be recorded by the registrar. For Affiliated Associations not having their own registrar or for Direct Entries, when sending birth records to the OBA Office, they must be listed, and a copy of the list included with the birth records. Upon receipt of a birth record, it will be recorded in the OBA Office. Players who cannot procure a birth record may sign a team roster upon producing a statutory declaration sworn by their parent or guardian establishing their age; the same to be accompanied by a certificate of a solicitor or notary public. [TOP] RP1.3 Residency Rules The basic premise of the Rep Division’s residence rule is that players are to play with the Local Association where they reside. Players must establish residence within the Local Association on or before June 30th of the playing year. Exception: a player, who (1) maintains Canadian citizenship, (2) has been a member of a Rep Local Association or Direct Entry for at least one year prior, (3) continues to be a member of the same Rep Local Association or Direct Entry, and (4) for any reason is living outside of the Province of Ontario, may continue to register with that Local Association or Direct Entry. Individuals may be granted permission by the Rep Registrar to participate in Rep play. Cases will be dealt with on an individual basis (i.e., exchange students). Affiliated Associations must establish territorial boundaries for Local Associations within the Affiliated Association. Affiliated Associations may also set policies applicable within their Affiliated Association. A copy of the boundaries and any related policies must be filed with Baseball Ontario. The Rep Division will consider the most recent copies filed by the Affiliated Association in any dispute. The Rep Division may, in its discretion, choose to not follow the Affiliated Association policy where it conflicts with the OBA Procedures and Playing Rules and/or the Procedures and Playing Rules of the Rep Division or the best interests of Baseball Ontario. In areas where no Local Association exists, the Affiliated Association may set policy. In the absence of such a policy, The Rep Division shall apply the following policy: The player may play for any team in the Affiliated Association without a release. In areas where there is a Local Association, but the Local Association does not have a team at the particular series, the Affiliated Association may set policy. In the absence of such a policy, The Rep Division shall apply the following policy: The player may play for any team in the Affiliated Association without a release. For the purposes of this rule, minor (e.g., 8U, 10U, 12U, 14U, 16U) is not defined as a separate series from major (e.g., 9U, 11U, 13U, 15U, 18U). If a combined minor/major program is offered by a Local Association, then the Local Association is considered to have a team at the particular series. The player is expected to return to his/her Local Association, unless a released is obtained pursuant to RP1.5. A player in the circumstances of e) or f) may play for a team in another Affiliated Association without a release where such team is the team closest to his/her place of residence. Closest team shall be interpreted as the distance from the player’s home to the relevant teams’ home baseball diamond as the crow flies. Where a Local Association has more than 1 team operating in the same series, the Local Association shall set policy and/or boundaries for such teams. The Local Association shall file a copy of such policy and/or boundaries with the Affiliated Association and Baseball Ontario. [TOP] RP1.4 Players Changing Residence Baseball Ontario recognizes that families move. As set out in RP1.3b), players must establish residence prior to June 30th of the playing season. In split family situations (i.e., the parents are divorced or separated), players 18 years of age and younger shall be deemed to reside where the parent(s) having custody rights reside. Baseball Ontario does not condone moves of convenience or falsified family splits designed to bypass the release process. Baseball Ontario, when considering the legitimacy of the family move or family split, may have regard to a number of factor(s), including, but not limited to the following: a statement from the parent(s) outlining that they actually live at the stated address; a copy of a rental agreement or proof of ownership of the residence; a driver’s license with the stated address; the name and address of the school the player attends; a separation agreement or divorce decree outlining the custody and access arrangements of the player; and/or Canada Revenue Agency Marital Status Change Form – RC 65 [TOP] RP1.5 Players Changing Teams, Releases, and Transfers Players are only allowed to be registered with one team at any time. Players registered with one team must be properly released before registering with a second team. This applies to all series. A player’s playing rights are held by: (i) the Local Association(s) where they reside as set forth in RP1.3 and RP1.4; and (ii) the Local Association where they played the previous season if the player obtained all necessary releases under this section RP1.5. At the Affiliated Association level, a player’s playing rights are held by the Affiliated Association(s) where they reside as set forth RP1.3 and RP1.4. Before a player may participate in a practice, game, tryout, evaluation, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights, the player must obtain the appropriate authorization as specified below. Where a player wishes to participate in another team’s out-of-province game or tournament and is registered with the Local Association that holds their playing rights, then permission from the Local Association and the Affiliated Association are required. Where a player desires to register with a Direct Entry or Local Association that does not hold their playing rights, the player must obtain: a release from each Local Association that hold their playing rights as set out in RP1.5 b); and a release from the Affiliated Association where the player resides, if they desire to register with a Direct Entry or Local Association outside of the Affiliated Association where they reside. Note: The request for a release must be in writing and the granting or refusal of a release shall be in writing authorized by the current management of the Direct Entry, Local Association or Affiliated Association involved. The foregoing does not apply at AAAA, 22U, or Senior where no release is required, and a player may sign with a team that does not hold their playing rights without a release as long as there is no equipment, uniform or financial obligation owing to their previous team. Where a player desires to participate in a tryout, evaluation, practice, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights, the player must obtain the releases required by RP1.5e). Release requests must be responded to and adjudicated by the Local Association or Affiliated Association, as the case may be, within 14 days of receipt. The Local Association or Affiliated Association may extend this due date by an additional 14 days should more information be required and must do so in writing to the person requesting the release. Should the request not be responded to within these parameters, the release will be automatically denied. Where a Local Association or Direct Entry grants a player a release, such release is valid and remains in full force and effect so long as the player continues to play for the Local Association or Direct Entry with which the player registered on the basis of having been granted such release. All releases must be in writing and cannot be conditional. Any player, for whom a Local Association is not prepared to offer a spot on one of the Local Association’s OBA teams, is deemed to be released. The Local Association is required to provide a written release upon request by the player. Any player, who has not been played by his/her team in 3 or more consecutive games, has the right to appeal to the OBA Technical Committee should the player’s team refuse to release the player. Releases shall not be valid until filed with the OBA Office. [TOP] RP1.6 Release Appeals Where a player has been denied a release under RP1.5 by a Local Association, the player has the right to appeal in writing the Local Association’s refusal to the Affiliated Association. The Affiliated Association will make a decision to grant or deny the release. The Affiliated Association’s decision is to be in writing. Either the player or the Local Association may appeal the Affiliated Association’s decision to the OBA Technical Committee. No appeal may be made until the Affiliated Association has made its decision. The appeal must include all evidence and correspondence submitted to the Local Association and/or the Affiliated Association and copies of all decisions made by the Local Association and Affiliated Association. The OBA Technical Committee will establish the time and place of the appeal hearing, which will be held within 30 days of receiving the written appeal and all documents required in RP1.6 c). The player (and/or his/her parent(s) or guardian(s)) and the Direct Entry or Local Association and/or Affiliated Association must be present in person at the hearing to provide evidence in support of their position. Failure to appear may result in a default decision. The decision of the OBA Technical Committee shall be final. [TOP] RP1.7 Offenses Where a player participates in a practice, game, tryout, evaluation, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights without the authorization(s) required by RP1.5, the player is subject to suspension by OBA. Any Head Coach, Assistant Coach or manager who allows a player to participate in any practice, game, tryout, evaluation, or other baseball activity with a team that does not currently hold the player’s playing rights without the authorization(s) required by RP1.5, is subject to suspension by OBA. No player, Head Coach, Assistant Coach or manager may approach any player for whom their Local Association or Direct Entry does not hold the playing rights with the intention of securing said player for their Local Association or Direct Entry. Violation of this rule will leave the player, coach, or manager subject to suspension by OBA. Where a director, officer, employee or other member of a Local Association or Direct Entry (who is not a player, Head Coach, Assistant Coach or manager) approaches any player for whom their Local Association or Direct Entry does not hold the playing rights with the intention of securing said player for their Local Association or Direct Entry, then such violation will leave the Local Association or Direct Entry open to a fine of up to $1000. In addition to or in place of any suspension authorized by this section RP1.7, any person or organization violating any of the provisions of section RP1.7 may be subject to fine or other sanction as determined appropriate by the Technical Committee. [TOP] RP1.8 Advancing to a Higher Series A player registered with a team in any series 22U or below shall be allowed to advance to a higher series of their own Local Association without affecting their status in the series and classification of the team with which they are registered. Any player, who advances, must play with their original team when the team they advance to and the team they are registered with have games on the same date. Where the original (rostered) team is competing in a weekend tournament, the player shall not be eligible to be used as a call up to the team they are advancing to until their original (rostered) team is eliminated from competition in their tournament. (Note: Any team knowingly postponing a game so that a player may advance will be suspended from the Rep Provincial Championships.) (Local Association shall be interpreted as the Local Association with which the player is registered.) In advancing under RP1.8 a), a player may only advance as follows: A 9U (including 8U) player may only advance to 11U. A 11U (including 10U) may advance only to 13U. A 13U (including 12U) may advance only to 15U. A 15U(including 14U) may advance only to 18U. Players who turn 16 years of age in the calendar year may only advance to 22U. For the 15U and 18U National Qualifiers only, OBA teams may call players across from other teams within their Local Association at the same age category, so long as that team is not also playing in the National Qualifier (e.g., an 18U team may call a player to their National Qualifier team from their 17U team (i.e. an 18U team of all 17U aged players), or their 18U A team). For greater clarity, a player may only play for one team in any one National Qualifier tournament. Where a Local Association has more than one team at a series, players that are on the lower classified team may be “called up” to the higher classified team. Where a Local Association has both a Select Division team(s) and a Rep team(s) at a series, players from the Select Division team(s) may be called up to the Rep team(s), provided the players comply with the residency rules and are the proper age. Where a player of a younger age is rostered with a team at an older age category (e.g., A 13U aged player is rostered on a 15U roster), such player shall be considered as a player of the older series at which he/she is rostered despite being of younger age and shall not be eligible to play with the younger team. Where a team wishes to use a call up player from a team that has been eliminated from any further competition during a tournament or a Rep Provincial Championship tournament or a Rep National Qulaifier tournament being held the same weekend, must have either the pitch count records or copies of the official game sheets for all games involving the call-up player. Any player, for whom the pitching eligibility cannot be confirmed, shall not be eligible to pitch. Once discovered, the penalty for violating any provision of RP1.8 shall be forfeiture by the team to which the player advances. The Series Chair shall determine what other appropriate steps will be taken in the circumstances. The Series Chair's decision is final and is not appealable. [TOP] RP1.9 Player Registration Each year, Local Associations will register their players and teams with the OBA through an online registration platform in accordance with policies and procedures determined by the OBA. The OBA will publish such policies and procedures on its website. By virtue of submitting a roster, the Local Association accepts responsibility for the authenticity of the information contained on the roster. The process may commence by a date determined by Rep Council which shall be no later than October 1st annually. Teams must submit a preliminary roster to the OBA Office through the OBA online registration platform no later than April 28th annually or five business days prior to the team's first game, whichever is later. Any team playing without having submitted a roster after May 1st shall be automatically suspended until such time as the roster is submitted. Rosters may be edited by Local Associations until May 31st. No changes to players on a roster may be made after May 31st without the consent of the OBA Rep Commissioner. At 19U Women’s, Senior Women’s, and Senior, submitted rosters may be edited by Local Associations until June 30th. At 19U Women’s, Senior Women’s, and Senior, no changes to players on a roster may be made after June 30th without the consent of the OBA Rep Registrar. At a minimum, the information to be provided through such online registration system will include: Name Date of birth Gender Address of residence (including postal code) Telephone number, and Valid email address Affiliated Associations and the OBA may review submitted rosters to ensure compliance with requirements of player registration and coach certification. Affiliated Associations and the OBA may remove players and coaches not meeting requirements from rosters. Teams with rosters not meeting the requirements of player registration and coach certification are not eligible to participate in Rep Provincial Championships and/or Rep National Qualifier Tournaments. No player may participate in a Rep tournament or Rep Provincial Championship without their name on a team roster. If a player, who is not officially on the team roster or on the roster of a team from which the team may call up players, participates in a game, once it is discovered, the Head Coach will receive a 10-game suspension. The suspension is not appealable. Subject to Baseball Canada eligibility rules, Senior teams attending Baseball Canada Men’s Nationals may add Intercounty Baseball League players as call-ups to their roster for Nationals. [TOP] RP1.10 Team Number A team must have 9 signed players and may have a maximum of 25 players who must be signed to a team roster of the series of the team entered. At 22U and Senior, the maximum roster size is 30 players. A maximum of 25 players can appear on a line-up card for any one game. Each team must have at least 1 manager, and/or 1 or more coaches, signed to the roster clearly marked “Manager” or “Coach” and who shall be responsible for that team. [TOP] RP1.11 Professional Players Registration Any player signed by a professional baseball club of Major League Baseball (MLB) or Minor League Baseball (MiLB), who has received his/her unconditional release or has been placed on the Voluntary Retired List by said professional club before June 30th of the playing season shall become eligible for reinstatement to play in the OBA immediately when he/she returns to his/her home city or town, or to the team he/she left to turn professional or he/she may play with any other team providing he/she qualifies under the residence rule. All applications for reinstatement must be accompanied by a letter from the Office of the President of the MiLB or a copy of the release from the applicable professional organization. Any player signed by an independent professional league or club who has received his/her unconditional release or has been placed on the voluntary retired list by said independent professional club who provides a written statement advising that they no longer play in that league before June 30th of the playing season shall become eligible for reinstatement to play in the OBA immediately when he/she returns to his/her home city or town, or to the team he/she left to turn professional or he/she may play with any other team providing he/she qualifies under the residence rule. A player shall be considered to have turned professional when he/she plays his/her first game, either exhibition or league, with a professional club. The release, voluntary retirement confirmation or written statement must accompany any application for reinstatement. [TOP] RP1.12 Eligibility Players are not eligible to register with the OBA for the then current playing season if they have registered with a non-recognized baseball program operating in the Province of Ontario for any part of such playing season. Players registered with the OBA for the then current playing season who register with a non-recognized baseball program shall be subject to suspension in accordance with section RP7.1. Individual players may participate with a non-recognized program outside of the province if they secure letters of permission from: (1) Rep Head Coach, (2) Local Association Executive, and (3) Affiliate Association Executive; allowing them to play with a specified team or organization. (All letters and information shall be forwarded to the OBA through the Affiliated Association). A player participating with a non-recognized program outside of the province without these approvals shall be automatically suspended indefinitely. Any further penalty shall be determined by the OBA Rep Council. Head Coaches, Assistant Coaches, managers, Rep Council members, Local Association and Affiliated Association Board Members shall immediately disqualify themselves for participation within OBA's Rep Division, its Affiliated Associations and Local Associations, if they are directly involved in any way with a non-recognized baseball program within the Province of Ontario. OBA-carded umpires are not permitted to umpire non-recognized baseball program games. For clarity, umpires are always permitted to umpire any game that involves at least one OBA program. Umpires who violate this rule are subject to immediate and indefinite suspension, pending review. Teams from non-recognized programs may not enter an OBA-sanctioned tournament. For the purpose of this section, the following terms shall have the following meanings: “non-recognized baseball program” shall mean any baseball program other than (1) a baseball program operated exclusively by the OBA, its Affiliated Associations, and its Local Associations, (2) a high school, college or university baseball program, (3) a baseball program operated exclusively by an associated member of the OBA, (4) a baseball program recognized by the Rep Council, or (5) a baseball program operated by an HP Independent Program in accordance with Section RP2.13 playing season” shall mean the period from April 15 through to the end of the Labour Day weekend. “registered” includes any form of signing or registration process and any player participating on a regular basis with a non-recognized baseball program shall be deemed to have been registered with such program. A player participating in another recognized program, including an HP Independent Program, shall have the right to sign only with: the Local Association in whose territory the player lives, or the Local Association with which the player was last rostered. In the case of a player participating with an HP Independent Program, in addition to the above, the Rep team with which the player signs must be classified at AA or below. Despite Section RP1.8 d. and e., a player with an HP Independent Program rostered on a Rep team AA or below may not be used as a call up to a Rep AAA team or an HP-OBA team. To be eligible to participate in a Rep Play-off, a player participating with an HP Independent Program must have participated in 60% of the team’s league games. If they have not, then the player is ineligible to participate in the Rep Play-off. OBA will enforce this rule on the basis of a challenge to a player’s eligibility and not proactively. If there is a challenge, the team will need to provide evidence of the player’s eligibility. Teams may apply to the Rep Commissioner for an exemption from the 60% requirement for extenuating circumstances (e.g. injury). The Rep Commissioner, in consultation with the Rep Deputy Commissioner, shall rule on the exemption request. Such determination is final. If the player is not signed by such Local Association, then he/she shall not be entitled to sign with another Local Association. Senior Players are exempt from RP1.12 g) [TOP] RP1.13 High Performance Programs There shall be two types of High Performance Programs recognized by OBA: HP OBA PROGRAMS (HP-OBA) Requirements: To register as an HP-OBA Program, the program must: be operated by a Local Association or Affiliated Association and roster with OBA through OBA’s approved platform, including paying applicable fees through its Affiliated Association; adhere to the age categories approved by Baseball Canada; ensure all coaches for each team obtain and maintain the minimum coaching standards for their age category for a AAA coach as published by OBA and for nationals as published by Baseball Canada; follow OBA Arm Care rules and use the OBA Pitch Count app in all games throughout the season. Opportunities: HP-OBA Programs may: compete in the Rep National Qualifier Tournament, if applicable; actively promote their try-outs on social media, websites or other public advertisements at any time; at 12U and above, sign players without the need to follow the release requirements of RP1.5 and hold early try-outs, subject to the timelines identified below. Timelines: To take advantage of the no release requirement and early try-out opportunities for the following season, an HP-OBA Program must: declare their intention to register as an HP-OBA Program for the following season by May 1 annually, otherwise the release requirements of RP1.5 would apply; not engage in recruitment or hold try-outs prior to: July 1 in the case of teams operating at 12U or 13U in the following season; or May 1 in the case of teams operating at 14U and above in the following season; complete their try-outs and announce their roster for the following season’s team by the HP-OBA Roster Deadline of: November 1 for teams operating at 12U or 13U in the following season; or The Wednesday after Labour Day for teams operating at 14U and above in the following season. For purposes of these timelines, “recruitment” is defined as any solicitation of prospective players or their parents by a staff member or authorized representative of an OBA-HP Program for the purpose of securing a prospective player’s position at a try-out or on a team operated by the HP-OBA Program. Releases and Playing Rights: Players who wish to sign with an HP-OBA Program for the following season: do not require a release under Section RP1.5 if included on an HP-OBA Program roster submitted by the applicable HP-OBA Roster Deadline; must obtain all releases required by Section RP1.5 if signed after the applicable HP-OBA Roster Deadline. If denied, an appeal may be filed in accordance with Section RP1.6; have their playing rights for non-High Performance Program play remain with the Local Association(s) and Affiliated Association determined in accordance with Section RP1.5. Classification and Competition: HP-OBA Programs: shall be classified AAA; may compete in any league they wish. HP INDEPENDENT PROGRAMS (HP-INDEPENDENT PROGRAM) Registration To register as an HP-Independent Program, the program must: not be part of a Local Association or Affiliated Association; annually declare to be an HP-Independent Program and pay the prescribed fees as determined by the OBA Board of Directors; not register any player 11U or younger on any team operated by such HP-Independent Program during the playing season. Opportunities: HP-Independent Program have players drafted to the team(s) representing Ontario at a Baseball Canada National Championship; have players on the Ontario Youth Team; have access to the OBA Pitch Count app and OBA OnDeck app at no cost; have access to any level of OBA certified umpires; choose to purchase OBA insurance. HP-Independent Programs may not compete in any Rep Play-off or Rep
